sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a futuristic cityscape featuring towering skyscrapers and neon-lit streets.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Built using procedural modelling techniques with custom shaders to achieve the cyberpunk aesthetic.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Final render exported at 4K with volumetric lighting and post-processing compositing.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A detailed 3D model of a medieval castle with hand-sculpted stonework and intricate battlements.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: High-poly sculpt baked down to game-ready topology, textured with PBR materials in Substance Painter.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Delivered as a real-time asset with LODs suitable for Unreal Engine environments.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a modern architectural building exploring minimalist concrete and glass design.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Modelled to architectural specification with accurate material properties and natural lighting simulation.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Photorealistic visualisation used for client presentation and planning approval.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a space station orbiting Earth, designed with modular docking bays and solar arrays.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Hard-surface modelling with kitbash components, textured to simulate weathered metal and worn panels.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Rendered in a deep-space HDRI environment with motion blur for cinematic stills.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of an ancient temple with moss-covered columns and crumbling stonework.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Environment built with scatter foliage, decal-based wear, and physically-based erosion maps.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Atmospheric scene lit with golden-hour HDRI and volumetric god-rays for dramatic effect.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a futuristic vehicle blending aerodynamic form with heavy-duty utility design.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Designed from concept sketches into a clean NURBS surface model then converted to subdivision mesh.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Studio-lit product render with a turntable animation for portfolio presentation.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a fantasy creature with organic musculature and intricate scale detail.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: ZBrush sculpt with dynamesh for primary forms, then refined with layers for secondary and tertiary detail.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Rigged and posed for a hero shot render, with subsurface scattering skin shader for realism.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of a modern skyscraper with parametric facade patterning and reflective curtain-wall glazing.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Procedural facade generated via Houdini, exported as geometry cache into the Blender scene.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Dusk render with interior light bloom and city-reflection in the glass panels.

sections.title: Overview
sections.text: A 3D model of an underwater scene with bioluminescent coral, kelp forests and caustic lighting.

sections.title: Process
sections.text: Volume scatter shader used for water medium, caustics projected via light texture maps.

sections.title: Outcome
sections.text: Final animation loop rendered in Cycles at 60 fps for use as an ambient background installation.

sections.title: Who I Am
sections.text:
Hi! I'm currently studying Mechanical Engineering at Concordia expected to graduate early 2029. I have a background in web development and information technology. Originally from Vancouver, BC, I moved to Montreal in search of opportunity and to make connections.

	My interest for technology started at a young age, growing up alongside the rise of the internet. Building my first PC was the moment that truly kickstarted my journey. It inspired me to run a small refurbishing side hustle, sourcing cheap parts from the second-hand market, assembling them into complete machines and reselling them for a profit. That hands-on curiosity carried me into software, where I completed a coding bootcamp at BrainStation and took on freelance web development projects.

	I then earned my CompTIA+ certification and landed a role as an Implementation Technician at F3 Technologies, managing POS, networking and security for small-medium size restaurants across Vancouver. During this time, I also worked at Capilano University as a Maintenance Worker. To which I enrolled in their Engineering Transition Program, a decision that brought me back to my deeper passion for designing and problem solving in the physical world.

	That program reignited something in me. The design projects, CAD modeling, prototyping and the whole process of engineering a solution from research to manufacturing, I found myself genuinely excited in a way I hadn't felt academically before. That's what brought me to Montreal where I can make use of my French as well. Whether it's drafting a design or getting into the technical weeds, I'm at my best when I'm doing both. I'm always eager to learn new skills, hone current ones and gain experience in industry.

sections.title: What I Bring
sections.text: Engineering: SolidWorks | Programming: C, C++, JavaScript, HTML, CSS | Development: Frontend & Backend, Git, MySQL | Productivity: Microsoft 365, Word, Excel, PowerPoint | Collaboration: Microsoft Teams

sections.title: What I'm Looking For
sections.text: I like building things from start to finish. This portfolio is one example, where I designed and developed everything myself. I enjoy the full process, from the initial idea to refining and improving it over time. Most of my projects reflect that same mindset of creating something practical and well thought out.
